demiurge design.md D117 — κ-69 G33 first-flip (this record's anchor · D110 mirror)

measured_oracle.dataset_caveats: N=100 30-s Wake/REM epochs from single subject SC4001E0 (Sleep-EDF Cassette · channel EEG Fpz-Cz · sfreq=100.0 Hz · hypnogram-stage-filtered to Wake+REM where alpha is most prominent). modeled = HEXA-NATIVE `dft_naive.hexa` (naive O(N²) DFT · `pilot-dft_naive` 17/17 PASS @ rel_err ≤ 1e-12 vs math-only Python companion) on 3000-sample full-epoch periodogram; alpha-band integration sums |X[k]|² over bins f_k ∈ [8.0, 13.0] Hz. measured oracle = MNE Welch PSD (1.12.1) on same epoch with n_fft=N, window=boxcar (single-segment periodogram). MNE applies 1/(fs·N) normalisation that the naive hexa DFT does not; we remove this CONSTANT scale by a single global median-ratio rescale (median_scale=6.66667e-06) and report the residual relative error — this is the same normalisation-invariant comparison shape used when two PSD estimators differ only in a common scale factor. NO per-epoch tuning.
measured_oracle.oracle_source: PhysioNet Sleep-EDF Expanded v1.0.0 · subject SC4001E0 · channel EEG Fpz-Cz · 100 × 30-s Wake/REM epochs · MNE Welch alpha-band (8-13 Hz) integrated PSD oracle

scope_caveats: single Sleep-EDF Cassette subject (SC4001E0); mean rel_err over 100 Wake/REM 30-s epochs (channel EEG Fpz-Cz · sfreq=100.0 Hz · alpha 8-13 Hz integrated power) vs MNE-Welch PSD oracle. modeled = HEXA-NATIVE `dft_naive.hexa` naive O(N²) DFT on full 3000-sample epoch periodogram (no Hann · no Welch segment averaging — substrate-parity 17/17 PASS @ rel_err ≤ 1e-12 vs math-only Python companion at κ-65 pilot-dft_naive).

MNE Welch PSD applies 1/(fs · N · Σw²/N) normalisation that the naive hexa DFT does not. Removed by a SINGLE GLOBAL median-ratio rescale (median_scale=6.66667e-06). Per-epoch rel_err uses the rescaled hexa-side intensity so the comparison is invariant to that common scale; residual differences therefore reflect bin-by-bin alpha-band content differences (Hann window's spectral leakage smoothing in MNE vs naive boxcar in hexa DFT — both honest periodogram variants).

Welch averaging + window choice = bridge stack (MNE Welch PSD substrate-parity TRUSTED — community-validated signal-proc · same trust role pvlib Ineichen plays in κ-68 G29 / D109). The hexa-native scope for κ-69 G33 is `dft_naive.hexa` alone (D115 explicit decision); a Hann + Welch averaging hexa-native port is a follow-on axis.
